ops.text: PRE-REGISTRATION E36 (Wave 1; σ_train-independent — GATE task, encoder variance is its own). Hypothesis: a fine-tuned transformer encoder (Ettin-150m / DeBERTaV3 / ModernBERT) or few-shot SetFit beats the incumbent sklearn gate on the frozen binary gate frame — "different architectures" per the user's directive. Data: the frozen GATE eval frame (steer vs no-steer; the existing gate frame the lexical lane uses, n≈1325). Infra: cc_steer/retrain/encoder.py (shipped b067ed3, paired-comparable via write_probs) — supplies EncoderSpec/train_encoder/score_frozen; the Ettin/DeBERTa/ModernBERT model ids are experiment-local here. Arms: Ettin-150m, DeBERTaV3-base, ModernBERT-base (each fine-tuned under a NON-CRIPPLED budget — explicitly fixes E1's 20-min/1-epoch caveat: train to convergence, early-stop on val), SetFit few-shot, a MANDATORY TF-IDF+logreg baseline, the rep-stats probe floor, and the incumbent lexical gate. Train locally on the now-free GPU (Modal H100 fallback if capacity forces it; .uv_pip_install never .uv_sync().pip_install()). PRIMARY: paired DeLong (cc_steer.instrument) each arm vs incumbent on the frozen gate frame — actionable per the card rule. The winner must ALSO beat the probe floor AND the TF-IDF baseline (else the "architecture" isn't earning its keep). VARIANCE SANITY: the best encoder arm gets a 3-seed spread (report SD) so an apparent win isn't a single-seed fluke — an encoder's σ_train is uncharacterized and may differ from the watcher's 0.008. $30 HARD CAP (mostly compute/GPU-time, minimal $ if local; Modal metered if used). Harness ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E36/ own venv (cc-steer[encoder] @ git+file@main). VERIFY: independent DeLong recompute from persisted probs; the beats-probe-AND-TF-IDF gate; the 3-seed spread; frame-integrity. No artifact ships — a win is a gate-recipe handoff (162a5dc).
